requirements.text: Serve a built-in dashboard at the API root path.
requirements.acceptance: GET / returns HTTP 200 with text/html content containing the TPS dashboard, while GET /docs and existing API routes remain available.

requirements.text: Keep the dashboard dependency-free and offline-capable after load.
requirements.acceptance: Returned HTML contains no external script, stylesheet, CDN, font, or remote asset dependency; all required CSS and JavaScript is embedded or package-local.
Dashboard JavaScript connects to /ws/tps for real-time TPS updates and does not introduce a new streaming endpoint.

requirements.text: Provide REST fallback and initial state loading.
requirements.acceptance: Dashboard fetches existing /api/v1/summary, /api/v1/sessions, and health/diagnostics endpoints for initial render and can poll REST if WebSocket is unavailable.
Page displays connection/API health, aggregate TPS/total call/token information, session rows or cards, recent TPS history/sparkline, and model/provider breakdowns when data exists.
Client-side code visibly marks the connection as disconnected and retries with a bounded/backoff delay without manual refresh.

requirements.text: Be responsive and accessible enough for local operations.
requirements.acceptance: Layout is readable on mobile and desktop widths, uses semantic headings/labels, and does not rely on color alone for critical state.
Existing health, sessions, summary, events, trends, diagnostics, metrics, docs, and WebSocket behavior continue to pass; root route does not shadow existing routes.
Dashboard content is either a small dashboard.py module/string or a single package-local HTML asset loaded by api.py, with no build step.

requirements.text: Add tests and documentation for the new root dashboard.
requirements.acceptance: tests/test_dashboard.py or equivalent covers the root HTML route and README explains how to enable and open the dashboard.

success_criteria: GET / serves the built-in dashboard as HTML from the FastAPI app.
Dashboard HTML is dependency-free and contains no CDN or external asset references.
Dashboard client code uses /ws/tps for real-time updates and existing /api/v1/* REST endpoints for initial/fallback data.
Existing API, docs, metrics, and WebSocket routes are not regressed.
README documents how to open the dashboard after enabling the API.
